DISCOVERY, 302, PLAINTIFF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES PC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, FOR CONTEMPT, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST BLOOM'S SALOON IS DENIED. MOVING PAPERS INDICATE THAT DEFENDANT, SHEILA DALTON, IS CURRENTLY IN BANKRUPTCY. (1) ON THE MOTION TO COMPEL THE EARNINGS WITHHOLDING ORDER AND FOR CONTEMPT: UNLESS MOVING PARTY HAS OBTAINED RELIEF FROM THE STAY, THE EARNINGS WITHHOLDING ORDER IS BARRED BY THE AUTOMATIC STAY IN BANKRUPTCY. SEE 11 USC SECTION 362. SECONDLY, IF RELIEF FROM THE STAY HAS BEEN GRANTED, ENFORCEMENT OF THE ORDER IS ACCOMPLISHED BY A SEPARATE CIVIL PROCEEDING AGAINST THE EMPLOYER UNDER CCP SECTION 706.154. (2) ON THE SUBPOENA DUCES TECUM: THE STAY MAY ALSO BAR POST JUDGMENT DISCOVERY IN STATE COURT. MOVING PARTY'S REMEDY, ABSENT RELIEF FROM THE STAY, IS TO CONDUCT A RULE 2004 EXAMINATION OR OTHER DISCOVERY IN THE BANKRUPTCY PROCEEDINGS. CONTEMPT IS NOT AVAILABLE BY WAY OF A MOTION TO COMPEL AGAINST THE THIRD PARTY DEPONENT. CONTEMPT PROCEEDINGS MUST BE PROSECUTED PURSUANT TO CCP SECTIONS 1209, ET SEQ. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE PRO TEM: NILS ROSENQUEST; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302)

calendar.matters:
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Sep-18-2014 Discharged

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Production Of Documents, For Contempt, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Bloom'S Saloon
Off Calendar Jun-12-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicolet E. Corliss, Esq. of Bradshaw & Associates PC, Tel. 415.433.4800, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: Plaintiff's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES PC's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, For Contempt, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Bloom's Saloon is denied. Moving papers indicate that defendant, Sheila Dalton, is currently in bankruptcy.
(1) On the motion to compel the Earnings Withholding Order and for contempt: Unless moving party has obtained relief from the stay, the earnings withholding order is barred by the automatic stay in bankruptcy. See 11 USC Section 362. Secondly, if relief from the stay has been granted, enforcement of the order is accomplished by a separate civil proceeding against the employer under CCP Section 706.154.
(2) On the subpoena duces tecum: The stay may also bar post judgment discovery in state court. Moving party's remedy, absent relief from the stay, is to conduct a Rule 2004 examination or other discovery in the bankruptcy proceedings. Contempt is not available by way of a motion to compel against the third party deponent. Contempt proceedings must be prosecuted pursuant to CCP Sections 1209, et seq. (Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order.) Judge Pro Tem: Nils Rosenquest; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
